mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy stances that increase equity valuation multiples compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation observations, or the mechanism loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate deteriorating manufacturing output and supply chain constraints, which reduce corporate profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in over 55% of consecutive decline periods, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which sustains consumer spending capacity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of activation periods, or walk-forward testing shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly expands aggregate corporate earnings potential, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger immediate market repricing of future rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation-spike instances, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample periods.
